channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Avalanche buries 3 skiers near Anchorage, Alaska officials say
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Three skiers who had flown by helicopter to a remote mountain range were swept away by an avalanche and likely killed under the weight of dozens of feet of snow, Alaska State Troopers said Wednesday. If the deaths are confirmed, it would be the deadliest U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance suggests other industries won't get tariff carve-outs
item.description: Vice President Vance on Wednesday said that industries other than U.S. automakers have asked for exemptions on tariffs, but suggested that the Trump administration would not extend further carve outs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs, according to internal memo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning a reorganization that includes cutting over 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care and other services for millions of veterans, according to an internal memo obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Babysitter accused of drowning child during bathtub baptism
item.description: A Muncie woman was babysitting a close friend's children at her home in Texas when she allegedly decided to baptize the children in the tub.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lava fountain height soars in latest episode of Hawaii volcano eruption
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Lava fountains from a Hawaii volcano reached heights of 600 feet (180 meters) during the latest episode of an eruption that has been pausing and resuming for several months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two men charged after Kansas City Chiefs fans found in backyard
item.description: Two men have been charged in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead in the backyard of a home in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Three Michigan brothers missing since 2010 are declared dead by a judge at mom's request
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved despite an explicit belief by investigators that their father is responsible.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The US is killing someone by firing squad for the 1st time in 15 years. Here's a look at the history
item.description: It was a punishment for mutiny in colonial times, a way to discourage desertion during the Civil War and a dose of frontier justice in the Old West. In modern times, some consider it a more humane alternative to lethal injection. The firing squad has a long and thorny history in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles County sues Southern California Edison, alleging utility's equipment sparked wildfire
item.description: Los Angeles County says it is suing Southern California Edison, alleging the utility’s equipment sparked January’s Eaton Fire, which destroyed more than 9,400 structures and killed 17 people in the Altadena area.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida jewelry heist suspect allegedly swallowed more than $750,000 in Tiffany & Co earrings, photo shows
item.description: A suspect allegedly swallowed more than $750,000 worth of Tiffany & Co. earrings following a jewelry heist at a shopping mall in Orlando, Florida, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mötley Crüe postpones Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health
item.description: Mötley Crüe announced they are postponing their upcoming Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, Fox 5

item.title: US paused Ukraine intelligence support: Trump officials
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe and national security adviser Michael Waltz both said the U.S. had paused intelligence sharing in addition to military aid.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Child with cancer given Secret Service badge during Trump speech
item.description: More than a dozen Democrats joined Republicans in a standing ovation for a guest of Trump, a child diagnosed with cancer who Trump said aspires to become a police officer. The child, named DJ, was held up by his father as Republicans and attendees in the House gallery chanted “DJ.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cocaine bundles found hidden in SUV floorboard at Presidio Port of Entry
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) — A 24-year-old man was arrested on Sunday, March 2, at the Presidio Port of Entry after officers seized over 25 pounds of cocaine hidden within the floorboard of an SUV, according to U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP). CBP says the seizure was made just after 4 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, NewsNation

item.title: Video: Child nearly hit by car after escaping Tennessee day care
item.description: “I can't imagine having to call a parent to say your child got out and was almost struck by a car,” said a witness.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, NewsNation

item.title: Pope increases physical therapy, marks start of Lent from hospital
item.description: During the morning he participated in an Ash Wednesday celebration, receiving ashes and Holy Communion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: These nuns advocated for immigrant rights for 40 years
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — At age 90 and 95, this Catholic nun dynamic duo have been “peacefully and respectfully” fighting for immigrant rights for over 40 years. And they never take no for an answer.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elissa Slotkin assails Trump's early actions, offers Democrats a way to fight back
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — First-term Michigan Sen. Elissa Slotkin accused President Donald Trump of driving up costs while pushing for an “unprecedented giveaway to his billionaire friends” in Tuesday night's Democratic response to his first joint congressional address of his second term.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Joann store closures: See the list of nearly 800 locations headed for sale
item.description: After more than 80 years, national fabric and craft retailer Joann is another step closer to being erased from shopping centers across the U.S. after the release of nearly 800 locations going up for sale in the coming months.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Florida man swallows $769K in stolen jewelry from Tiffany & Co.: police
item.description: A man was accused of stealing hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of jewelry from a Tiffany & Co. store in Orlando and swallowing the evidence.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WGN 9

item.title: IRS drafting plans to cut as much as half of its 90,000-person workforce, AP sources say
item.description: The IRS is drafting plans to cut its workforce by as much as half through a mix of layoffs, attrition and incentivized buyouts, according to two people familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge tells attorneys to stop being so secretive in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho judge is warning attorneys to quit filing so many sealed documents in the murder case of a man accused in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The IRS is drafting plans to cut as much as half of its 90,000-person workforce, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS is drafting plans to cut its workforce by as much as half through a mix of layoffs, attrition and incentivized buyouts, according to two people familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy John's is giving out free sandwiches. Here's how to redeem the limited-time deal
item.description: Sandwich chain Jimmy John’s is giving away $1 million worth of toasted subs on Tuesday to celebrate a new product launch.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: Idaho murders case: Bryan Kohberger judge ends 'pervasive' secret filings restricting what public can see
item.description: Judge overseeing Bryan Kohberger's Idaho student murders case says practice of filing many documents under seal violates the First Amendment and will end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Columbia professor slams university leadership as anti-Israel agitators wreak havoc at Barnard
item.description: Columbia associate professor Shai Davidai criticizes leadership at Columbia and Barnard as anti-Israel agitators once again wreak havoc on campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court makes it harder for EPA to police sewage discharges
item.description: (AP/KRON) — A divided Supreme Court on Tuesday made it harder for environmental regulators to limit water pollution, ruling for San Francisco in a case about the discharge of raw sewage that sometimes occurs during heavy rains.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: How to watch Trump address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to address a joint session of Congress for the first time during his second term on Tuesday, outlining the goals of his administration and how he views the state of the country.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: 9 states report measles cases as outbreaks grow
item.description: One of the most contagious viruses in the world is spreading around the United States. So far in 2025 there have been three distinct measles outbreaks and at least 164 cases reported to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention coming from nine states.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FAA investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said Monday it is investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Storms, possible twisters to threaten the South just as New Orleans celebrates Mardi Gras
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms with a threat of tornadoes are expected to punch through Louisiana and other parts of the South on Tuesday just as costumed revelers celebrate Mardi Gras with huge parades and partying in the streets of New Orleans and other cities in the region.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP rep introduces measure putting Trump face on $100 bill
item.description: First-term GOP Rep. Brandon Gill (Texas) has introduced a measure that would require all $100 bills to have President Trump’s face on them.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4
